title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How to Invest in SIP?
Investing in the market often requires time, expertise and the ability to monitor your investments to ensure that they are performing as expected. For beginners or for those wanting to invest in a systematic manner, an SIP might be a good choice. It allows investors to contribute smaller amounts at regular intervals while the investments are managed by professional fund managers and spread across different securities. Before exploring different ways to invest in SIP, let’s start with the basics and understand what exactly SIP is.
What Is a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP)?
A Systematic Investment Plan, commonly known as SIP, is an investment mode applicable to mutual funds. Under SIP, you invest by putting in a specified amount at specified intervals, such as monthly or quarterly. Instead of investing a lump sum amount, SIP helps investors to spread their investments over time, making the process of investing more affordable and disciplined.
Once you set up an investment in SIP, the amount is debited automatically from your bank account and invested in the mutual fund that you have chosen. This convenience makes SIPs suitable for investors who don't have time to actively monitor the market or manage their investments, and want to invest for the long term.
How Does SIP Investment Work?
With SIP, a fixed amount is invested in a mutual fund at regular intervals. Each instalment is used to buy units of the fund based on its value on that particular day. Since mutual fund prices change daily, the number of units you receive may vary from one instalment to the next.
This regular pattern of investing helps reduce the impact of market ups and downs. When prices are falling, the same investment amount buys more units, and when prices are rising, fewer units are bought.
You can use a SIP calculator to find the estimated corpus that you can create at an assumed rate of interest and after a specified tenure.
Benefits of Investing Through SIPs
SIPs offer a structured and practical way to invest in mutual funds over time. Below are some of the benefits that make SIPs one of the most preferred modes of investing
Rupee Cost Averaging
Instead of trying to invest at the right time, SIPs invest a fixed amount at regular intervals. When prices dip, the investment buys more units, and when prices rise, it buys fewer. This investment strategy is known as rupee cost averaging. This lps lower the average cost of investment in the long run.
Financial Discipline
By investing at regular intervals, SIPs help bring discipline and encourage regular investments. Automated contributions support help investors stay invested even during market ups and downs and uncertain time supporting long-term wealth planning.
Ease and Flexibility of Investing
SIPs are designed to be both flexible and easy to manage. Investors can adjust their contribution amount, pause investments for a period, or discontinue them altogether based on changing income or life circumstances. Once a SIP is set up, the investment process runs automatically through bank auto-debit, removing the need for manual transactions or frequent market tracking.
Where Can You Invest Using SIPs?
One of the advantages of SIP investing is the wide range of investment options it offers. Below are some of the areas where investors can invest using SIPs:
Equity Markets
Through SIPs, investors can put money into shares of companies across different sizes, such as large, mid-sized, or small companies through equity mutual funds. This is usually preferred by risk tolerant investors looking for high growth potential over the long term.
Fixed-Income Instruments
SIPs can also be used to invest in instruments like government securities, corporate bonds, and money market instruments through debt mutual funds. This option is usually preferred by investors looking for safer options with fixed returns.
Mix of Equity and Fixed instruments
Some SIP options, like hybrid mutual funds, allows invest across both equity markets and fixed-income instruments. This combination helps balance growth potential with stability within a single investment plan.
Market Indices
Investors can use SIPs to invest in portfolios that track a specific stock market index through index funds. This allows participation in the overall market movement without selecting individual stocks.
Specific Sectors or Themes
Thematic fund SIPs also allow investments focused on particular sectors such as banking, technology, healthcare, or broader economic themes. These options allow targeted exposure but may involve a higher risk due to concentration.
Tax Saving Investments
ELSS SIP investments are designed to support tax planning. These offer tax deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh in a financial year under Section 80C. But they also come with a mandatory 3-year lock-in period.
How to Invest in a SIP in India?
The process of starting an SIP is simple and easy. Here is how you can start in SIP
Decide the  Investment Amount and Frequency
Start by deciding on an amount you can invest regularly without putting pressure on your monthly expenses. It is long term financial commitment, so choose an amount you can contribute regularly. You can set up SIPs with different frequencies depending on the asset management company (AMC) and the platform, including daily, weekly, fortnightly, or quarterly
For example, if your monthly income is ₹50,000 and you can set aside ₹4,000 after meeting your expenses, you can choose this as your SIP amount and continue investing over the long term.
Complete KYC and Documentation
Before investing in an SIP, you are required to complete the Know Your Customer (KYC) process. This involves submitting basic documents such as proof of identity, address, and bank details. It is mandatory to complete KYC if you wish to start an SIP investment in a mutual fund.
Choose the SIP Date and Mutual Fund Scheme
The next step is to select the SIP date and the mutual fund scheme you want to invest in. The SIP date is the day on which your investment amount will be debited every month, so make sure you choose the one that suits you well. For example, if your salary is credited on the 1st of every month, you may choose the 5th as your SIP date to ensure sufficient balance in your account.
Online SIP Investment Process
With the emergence of online platforms, investing has become more convenient and accessible. You can choose from anywhere and anytime. You just have to choose an authorised investment platform, complete your KYC digitally, select the investment option, decide the SIP amount and date, and register a bank mandate for auto-debit. Once the setup is complete, your SIP runs automatically.
Offline SIP Investment Process
If you aren’t comfortable with the online. You can also start an SIP offline. Complete the KYC formalities, if not already done, by submitting documents such as PAN, address proof, and bank details to the asset management company branch or Registrar and Transfer Agents (RTAs), or through a registered financial advisor . After this, you need to fill out the relevant investment or SIP application form along with a bank mandate to enable automatic deductions for SIP investments, and submit the investment amount through cheque or demand draft to the asset management company branch or Registrar and Transfer Agents (RTAs), or through a registered financial advisor. Once the investment is processed, a folio number is generated, and you receive an account statement confirming your investment. This process can also be completed through the RTA office.
Key Factors to Consider Before Starting a SIP
Starting an SIP is a long-term commitment, which makes it essential to evaluate certain factors beforehand. Understanding these aspects can help you choose a plan that suits you the best.
Financial Goals and Time Horizon
Before starting an SIP, it is important to clearly define what you are investing in and how long you plan to stay invested. Different goals, such as building long-term wealth, planning for education, or creating a retirement corpus, require different investment horizons. Having clarity on your goal and time frame helps you choose an SIP approach that aligns with your financial needs and expectations.
Risk Tolerance Assessment
Every investor has a different risk appetite. It's important to know yours before choosing to invest in an SIP. Understanding how much volatility you are comfortable with helps you select an SIP that suits your financial temperament and reduces the chances of making decisions driven by short-term market ups and downs.
How Much Should You Invest in a SIP?
The ideal SIP amount varies from one investor to another, based on personal finances and priorities. Instead of focusing on a fixed figure, it is more important to assess how much you can invest comfortably while managing your day-to-day expenses.
Key Takeaways
SIP allows you to invest small amounts regularly instead of making a one-time lump sum investment, making market participation more manageable.
Once an SIP is set up, investments happen automatically, reducing the need to track markets or make frequent decisions.
SIPs help manage market volatility by spreading investments over time and averaging the cost of investment.
Investors can choose from a wide range of investment avenues, including equity, fixed-income options, market indices, sector-based investments, and tax-saving options.
Starting an SIP involves simple steps such as deciding the investment amount, completing KYC, choosing a suitable SIP date, and selecting an online or offline mode.
The most important factor in SIP investing is consistency. Even a small monthly amount, invested regularly, can support long-term financial goals.
Conclusion
SIPs provide a disciplined way to invest consistently and work towards long-term financial goals without the stress of market timing. Assess the investment options available and choose a SIP that aligns with your financial goals and requirements.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
How do I start investing in SIP for the first time?
To start investing in an SIP, first assess how much you can invest and in what frequency, complete your KYC, select an investment option, and set up the SIP through an authorised provider via online or offline mode.
Can I invest ₹1,000 per month in SIP?
Yes, you can invest ₹1,000 per month in an SIP.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

cost of investment. 4. Investors can choose from a wide range of investment avenues, including equity, fixed-

invested regularly, can support long-term financial goals.
